subject: Business Collaboration Opportunity
text:

I hope this email finds you well. I am Peter Kok, a Sudanese government official who has been involved in efforts to promote peace and stability in my country. Unfortunately, my work has not gone unnoticed, and I have faced persecution from certain quarters.

In recent times, I have been seeking opportunities for investment and relocation. My family and I are currently residing in Cape Town, South Africa, where we are exploring options for investing our resources in a new location.

I am reaching out to you in the hope that you may be able to assist us in this endeavor. Your expertise and guidance would be invaluable in helping us navigate the process of investing in properties in your country. If you are interested in learning more about how we can work together, please feel free to respond to this email.

I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

Peter Kok
label: phishing

subject: Notification Regarding the Estate of Mr. Mike and Carol Hall
text:
Dear [Recipient],

I am writing to you with great respect regarding the passing of my late clients, Mr. Mike and Carol Hall. As their legal representative, I have been tasked with facilitating the distribution of their estate according to their wishes.

As you may be aware, Mr. and Mrs. Hall were a wealthy couple who resided in the United Kingdom prior to their untimely death on December 26th, 2004, during the tsunami disaster. Before their passing, they entrusted HSBC Bank PLC with a substantial sum of US$15,500,000.00.

The bank has contacted my firm requesting that I present a relative or beneficiary of the deceased to enable the release of these funds in accordance with British and international laws. Unfortunately, I have been unable to locate any immediate family members or heirs.

In light of this situation, I am reaching out to you as a potential next of kin. If you are willing to cooperate, I can present you to the bank as the beneficiary, and upon successful completion of the transaction, you will receive 40% of the total sum.

To facilitate this process, I will provide you with detailed instructions and documentation to ensure a smooth execution. Additionally, I will share privileged information about Mr. Hall's account with HSBC Bank PLC, including the account number and secret PIN code, allowing you to verify the information at your earliest convenience.

I would also appreciate it if you could provide me with your confidential contact information, including your phone number and fax number (if applicable). This will enable us to communicate effectively throughout this process.

Please feel free to reach out to me at my email address: alex.given@yahoo.com. I will also share a private email address and phone number with you as we proceed.

Sincerely,

Barrister ALEX GIVENS
label: phishing

subject: Request for Assistance with Inheriting Funds
text:
Dear [Recipient],

I hope this email finds you well. I am reaching out to you as a highly respected individual in your country, having sourced your profile from a reputable human resource database.

Although we have never had the pleasure of meeting, I would like to introduce myself as Morgan Yormie Wimpoe, from Liberia, West Africa. My father, John Yormie Wimpoe, was a former Deputy Minister of National Security in Liberia. Unfortunately, he was falsely accused and killed without trial by the then President of Liberia, Charles Taylor.

I have attached a news article from USA Today (http://www.usatoday.com/news/world/2003-07-15-liberia_x.htm) that verifies my father's situation. Before his untimely passing, he instructed me to escape with our family to Johannesburg, South Africa, and left instructions for managing a diplomatic trust company holding funds.

I am now seeking your assistance in claiming these funds from the security company, which is sealed in a consignment. In return, I would like you to invest this amount in your country, allowing us to start a new life there. There is an exclusion clause in the original agreement that requires the transfer of the consignment to a foreign beneficiary outside Africa.

I have chosen to reach out to you due to your reputation and expertise in navigating complex situations. I believe your involvement would be instrumental in ensuring a successful outcome, and I am willing to provide any necessary assurances or guarantees.

The funds are valued at $32 million, and you will receive 20% of the total sum upon completion of the transaction. All modalities are in place, making this a risk-free opportunity.

To proceed with this matter, please include your full name and private telephone number. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

Morgan Yormie Wimpoe
label: phishing

subject: Notification Regarding Inheritance of Funds
text:
Dear [Recipient],

I am Amir Ebrahimi, an account officer at First Merit Bank in Swindon, United Kingdom. I am writing to inform you of a unique opportunity for us to collaborate on the transfer of a substantial sum of money from a deceased customer's account.

As the account officer responsible for Mr. Abdullah Saleh's (deceased) accounts and financial obligations, I have been overseeing his estate since his passing in 1999. A recent search has revealed that none of his next-of-kin or family members have come forward to claim the funds as heirs, according to our bank's guidelines.

I am reaching out to you because I believe your assistance would be valuable in facilitating the transfer of these funds. As Mr. Saleh did not designate a specific beneficiary at the time of account opening six years ago, we require an individual to act as next-of-kin for the purpose of this transaction.

To proceed, I kindly request that you provide me with the following information:

1. Your full name and address
2. Your direct telephone and fax numbers

Once received, I will initiate the necessary procedures to update our records and file your name in form A2ag as next-of-kin. This process typically takes seven working days.

I assure you that this transaction is legitimate, and we are committed to ensuring a smooth and efficient transfer of funds. Your cooperation would be greatly appreciated, and I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

Amir Ebrahimi
Account Officer, First Merit Bank
label: phishing

subject: Re: Unclaimed Funds Notification
text:
Dear [Recipient],

I am Sule Oketayo, Head of Treasury Department at Eagle Bank PLC in Nigeria. I am reaching out to you regarding an unclaimed account that has been inactive since 1998. The account, which was opened in 1993, belonged to the president of MIRA CONSTRUCTION COMPANY LTD and contains a significant sum of US$13,500,000.

After conducting a thorough investigation and gathering necessary documentation, I am now seeking assistance in releasing these funds. Due to the lack of next-of-kin or relatives claiming the account, I am looking for someone willing to act as the beneficiary.

As the bank's regulations stipulate that unclaimed funds will be transferred to the Central Bank treasury after five years, I believe it is essential to expedite this process. I am proposing a collaborative effort to release these funds, with you receiving 20% of the total sum. This amount can be adjusted to suit your needs.

To facilitate this transfer, I require your assistance in providing necessary information and documentation. You will also need to provide your phone and fax number for secure communication purposes.

Please note that all costs associated with the transfer will be covered by me, ensuring a seamless process. Once the funds are transferred to your country, we can discuss the disbursement of my share according to our agreed-upon percentages.

I understand the importance of confidentiality in this matter and assure you that it is 100% risk-free. I will not engage with any other parties until I am convinced that you are not interested.

If you are willing to assist me in releasing these funds, please respond to this email, and I will provide you with an online banking access code to verify the account balance using our bank's online platform.

Best regards,

Sule Oketayo
Eagle Bank PLC
label: phishing

subject: A Personal Appeal for Assistance
text:
Dear [Recipient],

I am writing to you today as the wife of Joseph Ejercito Estrada, the 13th President of the Philippines. My husband's popularity in the film industry contributed significantly to his success at the polls, and he went on to hold various positions including Senator, Vice-President, and eventually President.

My husband has been accused of several allegations during his time in office, including illegal acquisition of funds. Despite his efforts to clear his name, he remains detained without success. The current administration appears determined to undermine his achievements and freeze his bank accounts.

I am reaching out to you today as a last resort, hoping that you may be able to assist us in our time of need. My husband's situation is dire, with allegations ranging from economic plunder to bribery. His former colleagues, including Guia Gomez, have even testified against him during the impeachment trial.

I would like to share some specific details with you:

1. A longtime friend and ally, Gov. Luis Singson, has attested that my husband received millions of dollars in payoffs from illegal gambling and tobacco taxes.
2. Witnesses have come forward claiming that one of the Philippines' largest banks held millions of dollars in bribes collected by my husband. The bank's senior vice president even testified to seeing him sign a false name to documents withdrawing $10 million from a secret personal account.

I understand that these allegations may seem shocking, but I implore you to consider the context and the motivations behind them. My husband's legacy is being tarnished by those who seek to undermine his achievements. I believe that with your help, we can uncover the truth and bring justice to my husband's name.

If you are willing to lend a hand, I would be grateful if you could take a moment to review these allegations and consider how you might be able to assist us. Please let me know if you require any additional information or clarification on this matter.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,
Luisa Pimentel Estrada
label: phishing

subject: Business Opportunity in Precious Metals and Diamonds

subject: Business Opportunity
text:
Dear [Recipient],

I am reaching out from London, where I serve as a Foreign Operations Manager for a reputable financial institution. My name is Jim Owens, and I am contacting you regarding a unique business opportunity.

As part of my ongoing efforts to streamline our account management processes, I have been reviewing the file of one of our long-standing customers, Mr. Norman Anderson. As it happens, his account has been inactive since 1999, and we are seeking a reliable partner to assist us in finalizing the closure of this account.

The specifics of the situation are as follows: Mr. Anderson opened a domiciliary account with us in June 1988, which currently holds a credit balance of £29.3 million. However, due to his unfortunate absence since 1999, we require assistance in transferring the funds to a nominated overseas bank account.

I would like to propose that you join me as a trusted partner in this transaction. As someone with a proven track record and a reputation for integrity, I believe your involvement could greatly facilitate the process.

To proceed, I will need your cooperation in providing certain necessary documents and information. In return, I am prepared to share all relevant account details, including the necessary documentation required by our bank. Together, we can ensure a seamless transfer of funds to your nominated account.

I would appreciate it if you could respond to this proposal at your earliest convenience, indicating your willingness to collaborate on this project.

Thank you for considering my proposal. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,
Jim Owens
Foreign Operations Manager
